meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Students: Solving No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement analytics and master version control as a student. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional-grade AI application.
content.answer_block: To combat the lack of version control, integrate lightweight analytics into your codebase immediately. By tracking event-driven state changes, you gain a 'functional history' of your app's performance. Use Antigravity to scaffold your data pipeline and Claude Code to automate the insertion of tracking hooks across your project architecture.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of code that works, but you have no idea how you got here. No Git history, no branches, just a fragile folder of files that could break with a single keystroke. This is the 'beginner's trap'—the terrifying realization that your progress is as ephemeral as a terminal session.

But there is a way out. By treating your application's analytics as a source of truth, you can reconstruct your development journey and validate your features. We are going to transform your chaotic workflow into a professional-grade analytics engine, ensuring that every line of code you write is measurable, trackable, and ultimately, defensible.

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Establish a centralized analytics service to avoid hard-coding tracking logic into your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an analytics-provider.ts file that initializes a singleton service for event logging, ensuring it is modular and decoupled from the main application logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file analytics-provider.ts --task 'Initialize singleton analytics service'

steps.title: Implement Event Interceptors
steps.description: Use interceptors to capture user interactions without cluttering your core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware wrapper for my API routes that logs every request payload and response status as an analytics event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Inject middleware interceptor for API route logging'

steps.title: Build a Version-Aware Schema
steps.description: Tag every event with a version identifier so you can track how features evolve over time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the analytics payload schema to include a 'build_version' field that pulls from package.json dynamically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Modify analytics schema to include package.json versioning'

steps.title: Automate Error Tracking Hooks
steps.description: Automatically capture crashes as analytics events to serve as a 'pseudo-history' of project failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a global error boundary that catches exceptions and fires an analytics event before re-throwing the error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement error boundary analytics hook'

steps.title: Verify Data Flow with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your implementation and ensure no events are being dropped during asynchronous calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for missing await statements on analytics calls and ensure all events are flushed on app exit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude audit --task 'Check for unawaited analytics promises and flush logic'

pro_tips: Treat your analytics dashboard as a secondary Git log; if you can't find a commit, look for the feature usage spike.
Always debounce analytics calls to avoid flooding your network during rapid user interactions.
Use a 'staging' analytics key during development to prevent polluting your production metrics with test noise.
Implement a 'user-journey-id' in your local storage to trace a single user's session even if they refresh the page.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your events; map users to anonymous UUIDs immediately.
content.next_steps: Now that your analytics are live, commit your code to a private GitHub repository to solve the version control problem permanently, then integrate a CI/CD pipeline to automate your deployment.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the professional-grade setup to stop building in the dark. Your portfolio is about to get a lot more impressive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
